danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprites with cyber-terrorism aesthetics, corrupted/digital visual style
danger_signals.meaning: Doc Waruyama's faction actively spreading cyber-terrorism; player in hostile territory

danger_signals.visual_form: Sparking obstacles, electrified terrain, energy barriers
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental threats aligned with the game's electricity/Volteccer mechanic; danger zones requiring traversal skill

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinct enemy sprites with exaggerated proportions and unique visual designs
danger_signals.meaning: Major faction representatives; critical conflict points in narrative progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Character sprite surrounded by blue/electric visual effects during normal movement
safety_signals.meaning: Player character's inherent power state active; player agency and capability manifest

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items with bright, distinct visual presentation
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; safe zones or beneficial environmental interactions

safety_signals.visual_form: Solid, clearly-rendered platforming surfaces with consistent coloring
safety_signals.meaning: Traversable, non-hostile space; moment of safety in level progression

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Clean, technological platforming spaces with power conduits and digital infrastructure suggesting C-Life origin world

faction_indicators.color_identity: Dark/corrupted color palette (contrasting with protagonist's blue)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: EUREKA system manifestation; C-Life beings appearing in corrupted form
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Cyber-themed enemy designs with grotesque or exaggerated digital corruption aesthetics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Corrupted digital spaces, twisted technological environments reflecting Doc Waruyama's twisted mind and cyber-terrorism infrastructure

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Pre-corruption (backstory): Doc Yoshiyama sane, C-Life peaceful
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Implied through character design and narrative exposition; stable technological aesthetic in level design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Historical context only; no active world-state shifts during gameplay

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-corruption: Galaxy Gang spreading cyber-terrorism via EUREKA system
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Corrupted digital environments, hostile enemy placements throughout levels, technological decay juxtaposed with advanced infrastructure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Galaxy Gang enemies actively patrol and attack; C-Life beings manifest as hostile entities; world under threat requiring Pulseman's intervention

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Pulseman's electrical power channeling (active during gameplay)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Blue electrical aura surrounding character sprite; Volteccer charging/movement effects with discharge visuals
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains mobility and attack capability through electricity channeling; Pulseman can traverse power infrastructure and damage enemies with electrical attacks

environmental_storytelling.example: Level design contrasts advanced C-Life digital infrastructure with signs of Galaxy Gang corruption and sabotage; visual narrative of world under siege by cyber-terrorism
Galaxy Gang members positioned throughout levels visually indicate territorial control and active threat distribution across the world
Ubiquitous use of electricity imagery and power conduits reflects Pulseman's hybrid nature and his role as conduit for electrical power against a digitally-corrupted threat
Pulseman's appearance as half-human, half-digital being communicated through sprite design blending organic and technological visual elements

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Blue electrical aura visible; responsive to player input; electrical discharge effects accompany mobility
Electrical buildup visual effect; enhanced mobility preparation; ready state for rapid traversal or attack
Visible cyber-corruption aesthetics; predictable movement patterns indicating AI routine; colored/visually distinct from protagonist
Attack animations trigger; enemy sprite may display aggression tells (increased visual intensity, faster animation cycles)
Large sprite presentation; stationary or controlled arena positioning; visual design emphasizes faction affiliation and power
Attack patterns with distinct visual telegraphing; energy/electrical effects indicating attack type and magnitude

notes: Pulseman's world-state communication is primarily character-driven rather than dynamic environmental shifts. The game operates within a fixed narrative state (Galaxy Gang's cyber-terrorism is active throughout) and communicates tension through consistent enemy presence and corrupted visual theming. Visual distinction between protagonist (blue electrical aesthetic) and antagonist faction (dark corruption) is the primary mechanism for communicating political opposition. Limited sources restrict granular detail extraction; this analysis represents observable patterns from narrative synopsis and implied gameplay mechanics.
